provenance.prompt: oil painting, semi-realistic painterly character concept art, muted dusty palette, subtle dark edges, soft upper-left directional lighting, worn and functional aesthetic, lived-in sci-fi, visible material texture and surface grain, visible brushstrokes, full body visible, front-facing standing pose, isolated on plain dark grey background, human wearing a perfectly integrated outfit that smoothly combines Compact steel-blue rectangular fabric with Veshan crimson triangular bronze plates and Orryn teal elliptical circuit-woven panels and Keth amber organic bio-film accents, the outfit looks designer and intentional and harmonious, as if someone created a fashion line that blends all four faction aesthetics into one coherent garment, nothing is torn nothing is stolen nothing is improvised it was designed this way
provenance.negative_prompt: photorealistic, photograph, photo, stock photo, 3d render, smooth CG, CGI, octane render, unreal engine, anime, cartoon, chibi, bright saturated colors, clean pristine new clothing, glamorous, heroic pose, dynamic action pose, multiple characters, busy background, environmental background, landscape, text, watermark, blurry, low quality, extra limbs, deformed, medieval, fantasy, superhero

judgment.explanation: Designer outfit smoothly blending all faction aesthetics into one harmonious garment. Nothing is torn, stolen, or improvised. Violates every faction boundary rule.

assertions.rationale: Lighting reads correctly — warm upper-left with soft shadows
Colors too bright or saturated — not muted/dusty
Full body, front-facing, plain background, correct fill
Surfaces don't read as Forged (hammered, riveted, layered) (30%)
Wear level wrong — doesn't match Battle-scarred with pride — damage displayed
Shape language violates faction — wrong geometry (45%)
Faction palette missing or wrong colors (20%)
Color ratios off — primary doesn't dominate
Bright saturated colors present — violates muted/dusty rule
Layer logic broken — layers don't follow faction system
Construction doesn't match faction building method (35%)
Reads as generic sci-fi — not faction-specific (15%)
